The first true academy for instruction, the Accademia e Compagnia delle Arti del Disegno, was established in 1563 in Florence by the grand duke Cosimo I de’ Medici at the instigation of his court painter, advisor, and art historian Giorgio Vasari. The two nominal heads of the institution were Cosimo himself and Florence’s favorite son, Michelangelo.
